summary: Task 3 implementation is correct and aligns with Decisions 1, 5, 7, 8, 10, 11. All six TDD-anchor groups are covered (29 tests green in `:app:testDebugUnitTest`), the non-Hilt construction guardrail `nonHiltConstruction_instantiatesWithoutInjection` is present, `LogExportModule` is deliberately absent (class-level `@Singleton @Inject constructor` is sufficient, so the AC grep for `provideLogExportManager`/etc. is trivially satisfied). Module-boundary hygiene holds: `:core-runtime` / `:core-settings` are untouched, `TapCounter.kt` has zero `android.*` imports, no `ErrorLog`/`errorLog` calls in the new package (only one reference in a KDoc comment, which does not violate Decision 10). The `openOutputStreamForTest` internal var is acceptable under the same Task-2 precedent (crashLogWriter seam) — explicitly pre-declared in the review context as a deliberate deviation and mirrors the agreed 'форма на выбор имплементатора' stance. Findings below are low-severity polish only.

findings.issue: The `when` chain in `read()` has a fourth implicit branch — `exitCode == null && !timedOut` — which triggers when `DefaultCommandRunner` catches `IOException`/`SecurityException` from `ProcessBuilder.start()` and returns `LogcatResult(stdout = empty, exitCode = null, timedOut = false)`. That lands on `result.exitCode == null -> "[logcat unavailable: unknown]"`. This is consistent with the task's edge-case note ('…или отдельный reason — не пробрасывать IOException наружу') and is a reasonable rendering, but no test in `LogcatReaderTest` exercises this exact result shape — all three unavailable-branch tests (`emptyStdout_returnsUnavailableEmpty`, `nonZeroExit_returnsUnavailableExitN`, `timeout_returnsUnavailableTimeout`) drive a different combination. A future refactor that reorders the `when` arms or collapses null-exitCode into 'empty' could silently change the placeholder string without any test catching it.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add one more case to `LogcatReaderTest`, e.g. `startFailed_returnsUnavailableUnknown`, driving `LogcatResult(ByteArray(0), exitCode = null, timedOut = false)` through a stub runner and asserting `"[logcat unavailable: unknown]"`. One line of production behaviour, one additional test — closes the last arm of the branch table. No code change needed.

findings.issue: `openOutputStreamForTest` is exposed as `internal var` — this matches the Task-2 `crashLogWriter: (File, String) -> Unit` seam pattern that was explicitly accepted as 'форма на выбор имплементатора' (task-2 code-reviewer-1.json finding #3, deferred in round 2). The review prompt calls this out as a deliberate deviation; flagging it here only so the precedent remains visible. `internal var` makes the seam mutable from any `:app` caller, not just tests — which is slightly looser than a `private val` + `internal` constructor parameter would give, but removes a constructor-overload + named-argument ceremony that would be noisier in the `LogExportManager(context: Context)` secondary path.
findings.recommendation: No change required — deliberate deviation matches accepted Task-2 precedent. If a future audit tightens the Task-2 seam, apply the same tightening here for symmetry. Optional polish path: move the default opener to a constructor-default `internal` parameter with `private val` storage; keeps the public surface identical. Skip otherwise.

findings.issue: `LOG_DIR`, `CRASH_LOG`, `ERRORS_LOG`, `ERRORS_LOG_1` are duplicated as file-private constants here; Task 2 has `LOG_DIR`/`CRASH_LOG` (+`DISMISSED_FLAG`) in both `CrashHandler.kt` and `CrashState.kt`. Task-2 round-1 finding #5 already deferred the consolidation question to Task 8 (Code Audit) under the explicit implementation-hint 'НЕ выноси файловые пути-константы в публичный API'. This file adds two more copies (`ERRORS_LOG`/`ERRORS_LOG_1`), consistent with that policy — flagging only so Task 8 has the complete list of sites to consider if it chooses to centralize.
findings.recommendation: No change in Task 3 — deferred to Task 8, same as Task 2. If Task 8 centralizes, a single `object LogFiles { const val LOG_DIR … }` in `:app/logexport` (not public API, not in `:core-*`) can own the five names (`LOG_DIR`, `CRASH_LOG`, `DISMISSED_FLAG`, `ERRORS_LOG`, `ERRORS_LOG_1`). Current state respects the task contract.

findings.issue: `renderLogcat(source)` resolves the `CrashReport` branch to the placeholder string directly, skipping `truncateLogcat()`. Correct behavior — the placeholder is already a fixed short constant and has nothing to truncate — but the asymmetry between the two arms (one calls `truncateLogcat`, one does not) is easy to misread. `truncateLogcat` would return the placeholder verbatim anyway (it is well under 100 KB), so routing both arms through the same helper would be functionally equivalent and remove the asymmetry.
findings.recommendation: Optional: inline `CRASH_REPORT_LOGCAT_PLACEHOLDER` into a local val and feed it through `truncateLogcat` identically to the About arm, or keep as-is and add a single-line KDoc on `renderLogcat` noting that the placeholder is a fixed constant and intentionally bypasses truncation. Either removes the re-reader's 'why is this not symmetric?' moment. Not a correctness issue — `crashReportSource_logcatPlaceholder` already asserts the exact placeholder appears.

findings.issue: `AndroidDeviceInfoProvider.activeModelId()` returns `null` and `downloadedModels()` returns `emptyList()` as Phase-2.5 stubs, with a TODO comment referring to `NOTES.md`. The NOTES.md backlog entry (commit 487b1fa) has been added as required. The concern is that the live main-process `LogExportManager` (Hilt path) will emit `active model: none` + `(none)` in every real export today — until the ModelRegistry wire-up lands. `AboutScreen` (Task 6) and `ModelManagerScreen` (Task 7) will ship with that placeholder. This is an accepted phase deliverable (Task 3 explicitly says 'допустимо возвращать заглушки'), but the UX implication is worth surfacing: any Phase-2.5 `.txt` a tester sends back will have zero model information, which reduces diagnostic value for the exact bug the feature targets (товарищ's model-init crash).
findings.recommendation: No code change in Task 3 scope — the stub is explicitly sanctioned. Ensure the NOTES.md entry stays visible through Phases 3-4 (it currently does) so the wire-up is not forgotten; consider surfacing the same limitation once in the user-spec deviations table in `decisions.md` when Task 3 is marked Done, so the tester/dev channel is aware that Phase 2.5 exports show 'active model: none' by design.

findings.issue: `formatGb` uses `java.util.Locale.ROOT` correctly to avoid locale-decimal-comma surprises (German/Russian locales render `11,7 GB` otherwise, which would break `DeviceInfoCollectorTest.headerFormatting_deterministicFromStub`'s exact-match assertion). The fully-qualified `java.util.Locale.ROOT` is reached at call site; adding `import java.util.Locale` and using `Locale.ROOT` would be marginally more idiomatic and match how `String.format` is typically called elsewhere in the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Optional micro-polish: `import java.util.Locale` at the top and call `String.format(Locale.ROOT, "%.1f GB", gb)`. Purely stylistic; no behavior change.

acceptance_criteria_checklist.evidence: TapCounter.kt, LogcatReader.kt, DeviceInfoCollector.kt, LogExportManager.kt all present (glob confirmed).
Local run: BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 5s, 83 actionable tasks, test task executed successfully. Commit message cites 29 green tests (6+5+3+15).

acceptance_criteria_checklist.ac: LogcatReader uses exactly 6-arg argv via ProcessBuilder, no shell wrapping
acceptance_criteria_checklist.evidence: LogcatReader.kt:31-38 lists exactly 6 constants; argvShape_exactlySixArgs_knownPositions asserts size==6, positions 0-3 literal, argv[4] matches ^--pid=\d+$, argv[5]=='*:E'.
LogExportManager.kt:77 — `suspend fun buildExport(source) = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) { … }`. Same pattern on writeTo (line 99).
LogExportManager.kt:120-122 early-returns the placeholder for CrashReport; `crashReportSource_logcatPlaceholder` uses ThrowingCommandRunner that fails the test if invoked.
Grep for `import android\.` in TapCounter.kt → 0 matches.

acceptance_criteria_checklist.ac: LogExportManager / DeviceInfoCollector / LogcatReader do not call ErrorLog.e (Decision 10)
acceptance_criteria_checklist.evidence: Grep for `ErrorLog|errorLog` in logexport/ → single hit in LogExportManager.kt:37, which is a KDoc comment ('bounded by `ErrorLog` rotation') and not a code call.
No changes to :core-runtime in commit 487b1fa (commit stat shows only NOTES.md + app/ files). Whitelist unchanged. Commit message explicitly confirms :core-runtime:test green.

acceptance_criteria_checklist.ac: No new external dependencies in app/build.gradle.kts or libs.versions.toml
acceptance_criteria_checklist.evidence: Commit stat (487b1fa) shows only NOTES.md + 4 production files + 4 test files under app/. No Gradle-file changes.

acceptance_criteria_checklist.ac: Non-Hilt construction path (Decision 5): LogExportManager(context: Context) public, delegates to primary, all collaborators public-constructable
acceptance_criteria_checklist.evidence: LogExportManager.kt:71-75 — secondary constructor `constructor(context: Context) : this(context, DeviceInfoCollector(AndroidDeviceInfoProvider(context)), LogcatReader(DefaultCommandRunner()))` is public (no internal modifier, no @Inject-only). AndroidDeviceInfoProvider has public @Inject constructor. DefaultCommandRunner has public no-arg constructor. LogcatReader has public secondary `constructor() : this(DefaultCommandRunner())` (line 28).

LogExportManagerTest.nonHiltConstruction_instantiatesWithoutInjection (line 277-289): calls `LogExportManager(ApplicationProvider.getApplicationContext<Context>())` (no Hilt, no @HiltAndroidTest, no HiltAndroidRule), runs `buildExport(ExportSource.CrashReport)`, asserts non-empty output containing both `=== crash.log ===` and `[logcat available only via About export]`. Guardrail against internal/@Inject-only regression.

acceptance_criteria_checklist.ac: LogExportModule scope discipline: if present, only @Binds/@Provides for interfaces, no duplicate-binding for LogExportManager/DeviceInfoCollector/LogcatReader/CrashState
acceptance_criteria_checklist.evidence: Grep for `LogExportModule|provideLogExportManager|provideCrashState|provideDeviceInfoCollector|provideLogcatReader` in app/src → 0 matches. LogExportModule is not created yet; class-level `@Singleton @Inject constructor` is sufficient for Hilt to wire LogExportManager, DeviceInfoCollector, LogcatReader automatically. DefaultCommandRunner and AndroidDeviceInfoProvider are also `@Inject`-constructable — Hilt can use them directly without a @Binds module as long as callers depend on the concrete type (LogcatReader takes a CommandRunner interface, which means a module IS needed before Wave 2 main-process wiring, but Task 3 explicitly makes that module optional and Task 4 or Task 5 can add it). Non-Hilt path works without any module (secondary ctor passes DefaultCommandRunner() directly).

structural_invariants.note: Decision 10 is about call sites; KDoc reference to `ErrorLog` rotation as context for errors.log bounding is informational, not a code call.
TapCounter is pure JVM — no Android and no coroutines.

tests_reflect_production.TapCounterTest: 6 tests cover all named TDD-anchor cases: sevenTaps_withinWindow_triggersOnSeventh, gapOverTwoSeconds_resetsCounter, gapExactlyTwoSeconds_inclusive, gapTwoSecondsPlusOneNs_exclusive, fewerThanSeven_noTrigger, eighthTapDoesNotRetrigger. Virtual time via closure-captured `var t` feeding `nowNanos = { t }`. No Robolectric (correctly — class is pure JVM).
tests_reflect_production.LogcatReaderTest: 5 tests cover all named TDD-anchor cases: emptyStdout_returnsUnavailableEmpty, nonZeroExit_returnsUnavailableExitN, timeout_returnsUnavailableTimeout, happyPath_returnsStdoutVerbatim, argvShape_exactlySixArgs_knownPositions. Hand-rolled StubCommandRunner + RecordingCommandRunner — no Mockito/MockK. Robolectric because LogcatReader.read() calls android.os.Process.myPid() which is unavailable on plain JVM. Missing: `exitCode == null && !timedOut` branch (finding #1 low-severity).
tests_reflect_production.DeviceInfoCollectorTest: 3 tests cover: headerFormatting_deterministicFromStub (exact-match assertion on the full header template with realistic values), emptyDownloadedModels_rendersNonePlaceholder, nullActiveModelId_rendersNone. Hand-rolled StubDeviceInfoProvider with reasonable defaults; no Robolectric (by design — the collector class has zero Android imports and the provider is a pure interface).
tests_reflect_production.LogExportManagerTest: 15 tests covering every AC and edge case: headerContainsRequiredFields, allSectionsPresent_orderedCorrectly (distinctive markers + index ordering), missingErrorsLog_rendersEmpty, missingCrashLog_rendersEmpty, crashReportSource_logcatPlaceholder (ThrowingCommandRunner guards the 'not-invoked' contract), aboutSource_logcatPopulatedFromRunner, logcatTailTruncation_preservesTailAndMarks (200 KB input → tail preserved + marker at head), crashLogHeadTruncation_preservesHeadAndMarks (200 KB input → head preserved + marker at tail), errorsLog1Absent_sectionOmitted (no `=== errors.log.1 ===` divider), freshInstall_noLogsDir_succeeds (no FileNotFoundException on empty filesDir), emptyDownloadedModels_rendersBlankList, writeTo_happyPath_writesContent, writeTo_ioException_surfaces (via ThrowingOutputStream test seam), writeTo_nullOutputStream_surfacesAsIoException (null opener test seam), nonHiltConstruction_instantiatesWithoutInjection (guardrail for Decision 5). Robolectric + @Config(sdk=[33]) + deleteRecursively cleanup matches ErrorLogTest convention. No Mockito/MockK — hand-rolled fakes throughout.

cross_file_consistency.LogExportManager_calls_DeviceInfoCollect: LogExportManager.kt:80 → DeviceInfoCollector.kt:35 `fun buildHeader(): String`. Signature matches.
cross_file_consistency.LogExportManager_calls_LogcatReader_read: LogExportManager.kt:122 → LogcatReader.kt:30 `fun read(): String`. Signature matches.
cross_file_consistency.LogExportManager_secondary_ctor_invokes_: LogExportManager.kt:73 `DeviceInfoCollector(AndroidDeviceInfoProvider(context))` → DeviceInfoCollector.kt:32 primary ctor takes `DeviceInfoProvider`. AndroidDeviceInfoProvider implements DeviceInfoProvider. Match.
LogExportManager.kt:74 `LogcatReader(DefaultCommandRunner())` → LogcatReader.kt:24 `@Inject constructor(CommandRunner)`. DefaultCommandRunner implements CommandRunner. Match.
cross_file_consistency.LogcatReader_secondary_ctor_noarg: LogcatReader.kt:28 `constructor() : this(DefaultCommandRunner())` — called by no one in production code yet; exists as a public non-Hilt convenience (tech-spec implementation-hint). Future CrashReportActivity (Task 4) may construct LogcatReader this way, but Task 3's secondary path goes through LogExportManager(context) which passes DefaultCommandRunner() explicitly. Unused-but-intentional — consistent with task spec.
cross_file_consistency.AndroidDeviceInfoProvider_uses_Context_A: AndroidDeviceInfoProvider.kt:136-144 calls context.packageManager.getPackageInfo(...), context.getSystemService(ACTIVITY_SERVICE). Standard platform APIs, all nullable-safe through the provider's return types. Build.* statics resolved at method-call time (not instance creation) — safe.
cross_file_consistency.ExportSource_enum: ExportSource { About, CrashReport } declared top-level in LogExportManager.kt:27. Consumed by LogExportManager.buildExport(source) only. Tests reference both branches. No cross-file duplication.

decision_compliance.Decision_1_single_txt: pass — buildExport returns a single String; no zip; `=== section ===` dividers match tech-spec Data Models exactly.
decision_compliance.Decision_5_non_Hilt_construction: pass — secondary `constructor(context: Context)` is public, delegates to primary, instantiates AndroidDeviceInfoProvider + DefaultCommandRunner (both public no-Hilt-required) inside. Covered by `nonHiltConstruction_instantiatesWithoutInjection`.
decision_compliance.Decision_7_section_caps_and_direction: pass — MAX_CRASH_BYTES=100KB head-slice + `\n[truncated at 100 KB]` suffix; MAX_LOGCAT_BYTES=100KB tail-slice + `[truncated: head N bytes]\n` prefix. Markers fire only on real truncation (renderCrashLog / truncateLogcat both gate by size > cap).
decision_compliance.Decision_8_logcat_source_aware: pass — ExportSource.CrashReport → fixed placeholder `[logcat available only via About export]`, LogcatReader never invoked (asserted by ThrowingCommandRunner). ExportSource.About → live LogcatReader.read() + tail-truncate.
decision_compliance.Decision_10_separate_crash_channel: pass — no ErrorLog.e calls in any new file, ALLOWED_COMPONENTS not extended, core-runtime/.../ErrorLog.kt diff empty.
decision_compliance.Decision_11_no_redaction: pass — crash.log / errors.log / errors.log.1 / logcat all rendered verbatim. Only transformation is size-based truncation (which preserves content, not hashes it).

overengineering_and_dead_code_scan.dead_code: None. Every class and method is either called in production (LogExportManager.buildExport/writeTo, DeviceInfoCollector.buildHeader, LogcatReader.read, TapCounter.tap, DefaultCommandRunner.run, AndroidDeviceInfoProvider overrides) or a Hilt-injectable constructor. LogcatReader's no-arg secondary ctor is not yet called in production but is a documented public API for Task 4's non-Hilt construction path (tech-spec § Implementation hints). Not dead code — it's a forward contract.
overengineering_and_dead_code_scan.overengineering: None observed. TapCounter is a 36-line file with one public method; LogcatReader internal CommandRunner seam is the minimum needed to unit-test the placeholder branches; DeviceInfoProvider interface is the minimum needed to keep DeviceInfoCollector tests free of Robolectric. All three are justified by the TDD anchors.
overengineering_and_dead_code_scan.accidental_complexity: The `openOutputStreamForTest: (Uri) -> OutputStream?` var on LogExportManager is the only piece that could conceivably be simpler — a tighter form would be an internal ctor parameter with private-val storage — but the Task-2 crashLogWriter precedent was explicitly accepted as 'форма на выбор имплементатора', so this remains consistent with the feature-wide seam convention. Flagged low-severity in finding #2 with no change required.
